data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent safe-luge-nog/b4d4-3369-6dd2-41e6, THIS child (ba2c parent-CLEAR consumer) vs whole roster (908c link-consumer + ba2c parent-consumer)]

Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes; all clean (high-confidence, benign-reading filter applied):

1. Implicit shared state — `managed_refs` + `should_propagate_removal` are EXPLICITLY parent-owned shared state (parent What/Scope). This child only READS the gate/set; the reducer fold + serialization is parent in-scope work. Verified `managed_refs` does not yet exist in the tree (search: no matches) — parent builds it. No hidden/undeclared shared mutation. CLEAN.

2. Conflicting assumptions — gate semantics agree across parent + both children: peer ref present + locally absent => REMOVE/CLEAR iff in managed_refs, else ADOPT inbound; local-authoritative, no inbound-remove dual. ba2c ("CLEAR only when Jira parent in managed_refs, else ADOPT") matches 908c ("REMOVE only when in managed_refs, else adopt") and parent's `should_propagate_removal` definition. CLEAN.

3. Dependency gap — ba2c logically depends on the parent primitive/gate existing; the parent-child hierarchy (b4d4 builds it; both children carry parent_id b4d4) supplies the ordering, and parent Scope explicitly tracks the two CONSUMERS as children. No undeclared gap. CLEAN.

4. Scope overlap — ba2c touches the PARENT-CLEAR path (`_map_local_to_jira_fields`, the `emit_detach_clear` branch); sibling 908c touches the LINK path (`_diff_links`, new acli `delete_relationship`, batch_dispatch link REMOVE). Disjoint code paths verified: the `emit_detach_clear=True` opt-in (outbound_differ.py:542) and the un-gated CLEAR branch (outbound_differ.py:400-414) match ba2c's root cause exactly. Shared gate is common CONSUMPTION, not duplicated logic. CLEAN.

5. Ordering violation — ba2c and 908c are independent consumers (parent vs links); no inter-child ordering dependency; both gated behind the parent primitive. CLEAN.

6. Consumer impact — both children rely on same-pass inbound suppression (ba2c: replace the CLEAR-time suppression of inbound parent adoption with adoption when the gate denies; 908c: link-ADD echo suppression) on DISJOINT ref kinds (parent vs links). The `_OUTBOUND_TO_INBOUND_FIELD` map already carries parent→parent_id (inbound_differ.py:578-581). No cross-interference. CLEAN.

7. Residual references — ba2c references parent alias "safe-luge-nog" in its Design; confirmed b4d4 is the parent and 908c cites the same alias. ba2c's own alias is tan-elbow-mica. No stale/dangling reference. CLEAN.

Result: no high-confidence cross-child interaction defect. Parent + this child are consistent. G4 CLEAN (empty findings).
